title: Learned Positional Embeddings
concept: Modern architectures often use learnable positional vectors, letting the network discover the best coordinate representation from data.

exercises.prompt: Why do Transformer models need positional embeddings in the first place?

options.text: To help the model understand the order of words
To translate words into different languages instantly

exercises.explain: Without positional information, a Transformer treats a sentence as an unordered bag of words. We need to add position vectors to keep track of order.

cards.title: Letting the AI Decide
cards.body: We know AI needs to know the order of words. But instead of us hand-crafting a math formula to tell the AI where words go, what if we let the AI figure out its own custom map of positions?

cards.title: Learned Positional Embeddings
cards.body: Learned Positional Embeddings are special numbers added to words that start out completely random. As the AI practices on text, it adjusts these numbers to find the perfect way to track where each word is.

cards.title: The Rehearsing Dancer
cards.body: Imagine giving a dancer a stage with no markings. Instead of tape telling them exactly where to stand (fixed coordinates), they practice and discover the best spots to stand on the stage over time. They learn where to go through rehearsal!

cards.title: Learning the Order
cards.body: In models like BERT, the system starts with zero knowledge of word order. After reading millions of sentences, the embedding for 'first position' automatically learns to group near words that typically start a sentence.

cards.title: The Formula Myth
cards.body: Some think AI models must use fixed mathematical formulas to understand word order. In reality, letting the AI learn its own positional vectors from data often works just as well, if not better!

cards.title: How Positions are Set
cards.body: Comparing how fixed math formulas handle coordinates versus how learned embeddings discover them.

left.text: Uses pre-set formulas (like sine waves) that never change during training.

right.text: Starts random and adjusts automatically to fit the structure of the training data.

exercises.prompt: What makes learned positional embeddings different from fixed mathematical ones?

options.text: They use fixed, unchangeable math formulas like sine waves.
They are trained and adjusted using data, just like normal weights.
They prevent the model from learning anything about word order.
They are manually written by programmers before training starts.

exercises.explain: Learned positional embeddings adapt specifically to the training data, allowing the AI to discover the most effective coordinate system for the task.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Learned positional embeddings start with random values and get better through training.
exercises.explain: Learned positional embeddings start out random and improve as the model trains on data.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Learned positional embeddings are treated as trainable ______, meaning they update and improve as the model learns.
exercises.sentence: Learned positional embeddings are treated as trainable parameters, meaning they update and improve as the model learns.
exercises.explain: Because learned positional embeddings are just parameters (weights), the model can optimize them using backpropagation during training.

exercises.prompt: Identify the AI model that is lying about how learned positional embeddings work:

options.text: AI Model A: 'I learn where words belong by adjusting my positional vectors over time.'
AI Model B: 'My learned positional embeddings are static math formulas that never change.'
AI Model C: 'I start with random position vectors and refine them using training data.'

exercises.explain: The second statement is incorrect because learned embeddings are updated and changed during training, unlike fixed formulas.

exercises.prompt: How do learned positional embeddings acquire their sense of order and position during AI training?

options.text: They are permanently locked and cannot change during training
They are updated during training like normal model weights
They require mathematical formulas like sine and cosine at every step
They completely ignore the order of words in a sentence

exercises.explain: Learned positional embeddings act like trainable parameters (variables) that adjust during training, allowing the model to adapt and find the optimal way to represent positions for a specific task.
